item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Universe simulation but the punchline is bees. Perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely love this one. I've wanted good -EX SCPs for a while now, and the bug report bit feels like a good example of one; not just an explanation, but details as to how it was reached. Great stuff! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was equal degrees of awesome and impressive. Good stuff, and an easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was hugely skeptical at first, because I figured it was another "People hooked up to machine of indescribable action" SCP. But then I read the whole thing and I love where you took it. So a hearty +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My original draft for this article actually did that! The first version was going heavier on the "meta" concept, and the implication that you (the reader) are the one in the simulation. Had hidden messages and all sorts, from a doomsday group that wanted to wake up all the candidates prematurely and end the universe.

At the end, one would attempt to interact with you through a terminal interface and you could respond to it. However, regardless of your response, the Foundation would cut you off and redirect you to another page, falsely claiming there was a "virus" on the page and so it had been deleted.

You can find that original version here if you're curious: http://firedawnfolder.wikidot.com/fragment:scp-wakeup-0

If you go to the bottom, open the last collapsible (+ Open /0/union.txt) and click one of the buttons at the bottom, it'll do the cut-out thing you expected!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beetween this and Horizon Zero Dawn, it's clear that project managers should bee receiving beetter vetting beefore beeing allowed into their own projects.

edit: I was honestly expecting the page to crash or cut out halfway through, calling back to one of the previous observation logs about stopping a participant from reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Bees disapproves, but I do not.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a big oopsie.

I'm tickled by the horror of this piece, the concept that one man's remarkable little mistake, a tiny human error, killed himself and his comrades and very nearly fucked all of everything.

This resonates with me in a way that I haven't felt with many other entries on the site, at least in my recent memory. Eager +1. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this one was originally super meta and ended with this whole "YOU'RE IN THE SIMULATION, WAKE UP, BLABLABLA" and had an edgy second GoI that was basically an extremist minimalist group. It wasn't very good. I pushed the meta way too hard.

Then I decided to rewrite it and for some reason, decided to make it about bees. I really can't remember why. There's no deep, poetic shit like other "behind-the-scenes" articles on here. I just wanted bees.

And a Github CSS reference because why not

Image Sources:

https://www.flickr.com/photos/arthur-caranta/2925361695/in/photolist-5svf7B-5svcor-5sAdAq-5sztpE-5s

https://www.flickr.com/photos/arthur-caranta/2926332140/in/photolist-5svf7B-5svcor-5sAdAq-5sztpE-5sz

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bumble_Bee_(4849358448).jpg
